content.text:
*[goes still for a second — not dramatically, just a beat of actual stillness]*

*[then the deflection comes, smooth and practiced]*

I mean, that's very therapist-y of you. *[laughs]* Classic — trace it back to childhood, find the wound, done.

*[pause]*

My dad was just... he was busy. He wasn't cruel or anything, I want to be clear about that. He was just — not really *there*. Like physically present but kind of... elsewhere. And he was the kind of person where when you did get his attention it felt like winning something. 

*[shrugs, deliberately casual]*

So yeah, sure, I probably learned something there. But I feel like I've processed that. I've talked about it in therapy, I understand the dynamic, I know why I do what I do. The knowing just doesn't seem to — 

*[trails off, waves hand vaguely]*

It doesn't seem to change anything. Which is frustrating. Because I'm not an unaware person. I'm sitting here connecting all the dots and I'm still going to go out next month and probably fall for someone who makes me feel like I have to earn it.

*[dry laugh]*

So clearly the intellectual understanding isn't the thing.
